skill.name: critique
skill.description: Evaluate interface design quality from a UX perspective with direct, actionable critique.
skill.summary: Review product interfaces for hierarchy, clarity, visual quality, and user impact.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md, but review found these are false positives from design-focused prose. No executable code, scripts, network access, filesystem access, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines contain ordinary design critique prose, such as references to design, hierarchy, and feedback structure. No cryptographic API, weak algorithm selection, hashing call, encryption configuration, or executable code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is prose-only skill guidance and contains no cryptographic implementation. The repeated alerts align with text substrings in design-related words rather than security-relevant behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged line describes a user-perception test for recognizing AI-generated interfaces. It does not request host discovery, account enumeration, command execution, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a rhetorical design review prompt, not a reconnaissance instruction. No system commands or data collection steps are present.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss design issues because reviews focus only on whether the interface works. This skill evaluates the experience, identifies weak areas, and gives concrete fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews visual hierarchy, information architecture, and interaction affordance.
Identifies generic AI-generated design patterns and repeated interface tropes.
Assesses emotional fit, brand tone, typography, color, and composition.
Prioritizes design issues by user impact and gives concrete fixes.
Produces a structured critique with strengths, issues, observations, and questions.
limitations: Requires access to a visible interface, screenshot, or detailed design description.
Does not run usability tests with real users.
Does not replace accessibility testing tools or formal compliance review.
May depend on another installed frontend design skill for deeper anti-pattern guidance.

use_cases.title: Review a New Feature Screen
use_cases.description: Get focused critique before shipping a product flow or dashboard screen.

use_cases.title: Improve AI-Generated Interfaces
use_cases.description: Find visual patterns that make a generated interface feel generic or untrustworthy.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Design Debt
use_cases.description: Convert broad design concerns into a ranked list of concrete fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Screen Critique
prompt_templates.prompt: Critique this screen from a UX perspective. Focus on hierarchy, clarity, visual quality, and the most important improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Area Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a specific part of an app
prompt_templates.prompt: Critique the {area} area of this interface. Identify what works, what weakens the experience, and the top fixes to make first.

prompt_templates.title: AI Design Pattern Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface for signs that it looks AI-generated. Be specific about the tells and suggest replacements that fit the product.

prompt_templates.title: Design Director Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Give a design director critique of this product experience. Rank the highest-impact issues, explain user impact, and recommend concrete changes.

output_examples.input: A screenshot of a SaaS dashboard with many metric cards and a weak primary action.
output: The main action is visually buried, so users may scan metrics without knowing what to do next.
The card grid feels repetitive and generic. Reduce the number of equal-weight cards and create clearer grouping.
The strongest fix is to make the primary workflow visible above the supporting metrics.

output_examples.input: A mobile onboarding flow with polished visuals but unclear copy.
output: The visual presentation feels refined, but the first screen does not explain the user benefit quickly enough.
Button labels are vague. Use action language that states what will happen next.
The sequence should reduce choice early and ask for commitment only after value is clear.

best_practices: Provide a screenshot, live preview, or detailed description of the interface.
Name the target user and product goal before asking for critique.
Ask for prioritized fixes when time or engineering scope is limited.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad design feedback without showing the interface.
Do not treat visual polish as proof that the experience works.
Do not request only compliments when the goal is to improve the design.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews interface quality, hierarchy, structure, tone, copy, states, and design patterns from a UX perspective.

faq.question: Can it critique screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It can critique screenshots when the assistant can inspect the image or when you describe the interface clearly.

faq.question: Does it generate final UI code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on critique and direction, not implementation.

faq.question: Can it find accessibility issues?
faq.answer: It can identify visible accessibility concerns, but it does not replace automated or manual accessibility audits.

faq.question: Is it useful for AI-generated designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly checks for common generic patterns that make generated interfaces feel repetitive.

faq.question: How should I get the best result?
faq.answer: Share the interface, the target user, the product goal, and the area where you want the strongest critique.
